descriptions: The Jarushi and Qarajah families had a blood feud that ended with a ceasefire in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: The Qarajah crime family, also originating from Ramla, used to have a long time blood feud with the Jarushis, resulting in the death of 24 people in the 1990. The feud ended when the Qarajahs were forced by the government to have a cease fire agreement whereby they will leave Ramla to settle in any other Arab settlement.

descriptions: The feud ended with a ceasefire agreement between the two families.
edges.evidence_quote: The feud ended when the Qarajahs were forced by the government to have a cease fire agreement whereby they will leave Ramla to settle in any other Arab settlement.

Al Salam 313.description: Political organization and criminal gang of conservative Shia Iraqis in Western Europe, founded by Mohammed Bunia in Germany. Based on the ideology of the Iraqi Shia militia Sarāyā al-Salām, it threatens secular or Western-living Iraqis in Europe. Members are known in Germany, Sweden, Denmark, and the Netherlands.

descriptions: Al Salam 313 has pledged allegiance to the Popular Mobilization Forces.
edges.evidence_quote: They have pledged allegiance to the Popular Mobilization Forces, a branch of the Iraqi Armed Forces without being under control of the Iraqi government.

descriptions: Al Salam 313 is ideologically based on Sarāyā al-Salām.
edges.evidence_quote: The Salam 313 has members in Central and North Europe and is based on the ideology of the Iraqi Shia militia Sarāyā al-Salām (former Mahdi Army).

edges.evidence_quote: The Blinders' dominance came about from beating rivals, including the "Sloggers", "a pugilistic term for someone who could strike a heavy blow in the ring", whom they fought for territory in Birmingham and its surrounding districts.

'Ndrina Paparo.description: A 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Isola Capo Rizzuto, Calabria, with operations in the Milan area. It is allied with the Nicoscia, Arena, and Barbaro families. Members have been involved in mafia association, extortion, arms trafficking, and public works infiltration, including high-speed railway projects. The clan was targeted in the 2009 'Isola' operation, leading to arrests and asset seizures.

descriptions: Paparo are allies of Arena.
edges.evidence_quote: i Paparo, alleati dei Nicoscia e degli Arena

descriptions: The Barbaro are linked to the Paparo 'ndrina.

Universal Aryan Brotherhood.description: Neo-Nazi white supremacist prison gang in the United States, primarily based in Oklahoma. Founded in 1993 by Wilhelm Mast within the Oklahoma Department of Corrections. Operates both inside and outside prisons, with members in federal custody and several states. Engages in racketeering, drug trafficking, murder, and other crimes.

descriptions: UAB is allied with Aryan Brotherhood of Mississippi per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Aryan Brotherhood of Mississippi

descriptions: UAB has Aryan Circle as a rival per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Aryan Circle, Oklahoma Irish Mob Gang

descriptions: UAB killed and dismembered members of the Irish Mob in an ongoing war.
edges.evidence_quote: Sometime around September 2015, 6 members of the rival Irish Mob, a notorious Irish-American organized crime group, were killed beheaded, dismembered by Universal Aryan Brotherhood gang members in part of an ongoing war between both gangs.

descriptions: UAB has Oklahoma Irish Mob Gang as a rival per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Aryan Circle, Oklahoma Irish Mob Gang

Genovese crime family New Jersey faction.description: The New Jersey faction of the Genovese crime family is a group of Italian-American mobsters controlling organized crime in New Jersey. It is divided into crews led by caporegimes involved in labor racketeering, illegal gambling, loansharking, and extortion. The faction has maintained a strong presence in Northern Jersey since the Prohibition era, with notable members including Guarino Moretti, Gerardo Catena, and Louis Manna. It remains active as of the 2000s.

ETA.description: ETA was an armed Basque nationalist and separatist organization active in Spain and France from 1959 to 2018. Founded during Francoist Spain by Basque nationalist students, it evolved into a clandestine paramilitary group conducting assassinations, bombings, and kidnappings. The group killed over 800 people and was classified as a terrorist organization by multiple countries. It announced a definitive end to armed activity in 2011 and formally dissolved in 2018.

descriptions: These death squads were set up to fight ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: death squads, including the Batallón Vasco Español, Triple A, Grupos Antiterroristas de Liberación (GAL) were illegally set up by officials within the Spanish government to fight ETA.

descriptions: ETA was founded by students frustrated with the Basque Nationalist Party's moderation.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA was founded on 31 July 1959 as Euskadi Ta Askatasuna by students frustrated by the moderate stance of the Basque Nationalist Party.

descriptions: Batallón Vasco Español was a death squad that preceded GAL in targeting ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: GAL activities were a follow-up of similar dirty war actions by death squads, actively supported by members of Spanish security forces and secret services, using names such as Batallón Vasco Español active from 1975 to 1981.

descriptions: Batasuna is listed as an ally of ETA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna

descriptions: Herri Batasuna is listed as an ally of ETA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Herri Batasuna

descriptions: Batasuna is considered the political wing of ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: Batasuna's political status was controversial. It was considered to be the political wing of ETA.

descriptions: ETA shared intelligence and provided training to the Breton Liberation Front.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA had extensive ties with the Breton Liberation Front – both organizations shared intelligence, and Breton revolutionaries received training from ETA in the Basque movement's clandestine camps in the Pyrenees.

descriptions: ETA and Breton Revolutionary Army teamed up to steal explosives.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1999 ETA militants teamed up with the (now self-dissolved) Breton Revolutionary Army to steal explosives from magazines in Brittany.

descriptions: ETA originated from the student group Ekin.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA grew out of a student group called Ekin, founded in the early 1950s

descriptions: ETA-V was a faction of Basque nationalists that split from ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: Basque nationalists who refused to follow the tenets of Marxism–Leninism and who sought to create a united front appeared as ETA-V

descriptions: ETA VII faction integrated into Euskadiko Ezkerra after accepting amnesty.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA VII accepted this partial amnesty granted by the now democratic Spanish government and integrated into the political party Euskadiko Ezkerra

descriptions: FLB-ARB is listed as an ally of ETA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure , Criminal elements within Republika Srpska (supply of arms)

descriptions: ETA exchanged weapons and provided logistical support to FP-25.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA exchanged weapons, explosives and provided logistical support to the Forças Populares 25 de Abril (FP-25), a Portuguese far-left terrorist group.

descriptions: GAL targeted ETA members in a dirty war, killing ETA exiles.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1980s, a 'dirty war' ensued using the Grupos Antiterroristas de Liberación (GAL, 'Antiterrorist Liberation Groups'), a paramilitary group which billed themselves as counter-terrorist, active between 1983 and 1987. The GAL's stated mission was to avenge every ETA killing with another killing of ETA exiles

descriptions: The text mentions ETA and GAL as sources of political violence, implying they are opponents.
edges.evidence_quote: These were the first systematic demonstrations in the Basque Country against political violence, whether by ETA or by GAL.

descriptions: GAL was a state-supported death squad that targeted ETA members, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: GAL , about the journalistic research leading to the uncovering of the state-supported GAL.

descriptions: KAS was an ETA-affiliated party within Herri Batasuna.
edges.evidence_quote: One of the parties within Herri Batasuna, ETA-affiliated KAS

edges.target: Liga de Unidade e Ação Revolucionária
descriptions: LUAR brokered weapons purchases for ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1960s, the Portuguese group Liga de Unidade e Ação Revolucionária (LUAR), which was fighting the dictatorship, brokered the contacts that allowed ETA to purchase weapons in the then Czechoslovak Republic.

descriptions: MLVN was a Basque party connected to ETA that helped create Herri Batasuna.
edges.evidence_quote: Basque parties connected to ETA such as KAS and the MLVN created a new far-left Herri Batasuna coalition

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure , Criminal elements within Republika Srpska (supply of arms)

descriptions: ETA and IRA had fraternal contacts and parallel struggles.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA was known to have had 'fraternal' contacts with the Provisional Irish Republican Army; the two groups have both, at times, characterized their struggles as parallel.

descriptions: Criminal elements within Republika Srpska supplied arms to ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure , Criminal elements within Republika Srpska (supply of arms)

descriptions: ETA received funding and weapons from associates of Radovan Karadzic in Republika Srpska.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA received funding and weapons from associates of Radovan Karadzic within the Republika Srpska, in the aftermath of the Yugoslav Wars.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure , Criminal elements within Republika Srpska (supply of arms)

descriptions: ETA had contacts with FARC, including meetings and training exchanges.
edges.evidence_quote: The Colombian government stated that there are contacts between ETA and the Colombian guerrillas FARC.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure , Criminal elements within Republika Srpska (supply of arms)

descriptions: ETA cooperated with Terra Lliure, which based itself off ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA cooperated with the Catalan nationalist movement, which had its own far-left separatist organization that based itself off ETA – Terra Lliure

descriptions: Terra Lliure was an ally of ETA; they coordinated actions and supported each other.
edges.evidence_quote: A Catalan revolutionary organization that based itself off ETA, Terra Lliure, was an ally of ETA, and both organizations coordinated their actions and supported each other.

descriptions: These death squads were set up to fight ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: death squads, including the Batallón Vasco Español, Triple A, Grupos Antiterroristas de Liberación (GAL) were illegally set up by officials within the Spanish government to fight ETA.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: NORAID (Irish Americans) , Libya , PLO , ETA , FARC

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: NORAID (Irish Americans), Libya, PLO, ETA, FARC

edges.evidence_quote: and, in Spain, the Basque left-wing nationalist group ETA

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Armed groups ELN , Euskadi Ta Askatasuna

Maceo Organization.description: The Maceo Organization was a criminal syndicate based in Galveston, Texas, founded around 1927 by brothers Sam and Rosario Maceo. It controlled illegal gambling, prostitution, bootlegging, and racketeering in the city, and wielded significant political influence. The organization operated from the 1920s until the 1980s.

descriptions: Quinn mentored and partnered with the Maceo brothers, who later succeeded him.
edges.evidence_quote: Quinn became a mentor, and then a partner, to Sam and Rosario Maceo , who later took over the Galveston underworld.

descriptions: The Maceo Syndicate, successors to the Beach Gang, ordered the assassination of Downtown Gang member George Musey.
edges.evidence_quote: George Musey was assassinated through orders by the Beach Gang's successors, the Maceo Syndicate

descriptions: The Maceo brothers were initially associated with the Beach Gang before taking control.
edges.evidence_quote: The Maceo brothers got their start by being associated with the Beach Gang

descriptions: The Maceo Organization drove the Downtown Gang out of the city.
edges.evidence_quote: By the 1930s, the brothers eventually ran the Downtown Gang away from operating on the city

descriptions: The Fertitta Group succeeded the Maceo Organization after the Maceo brothers' deaths.
edges.evidence_quote: After Sam and Rose's deaths in the early 1950s, the brothers were succeeded by the Fertitta Group

Pittsburgh crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, formed in the late 1880s under Salvatore Catanzaro. It rose to prominence during Prohibition and was led for decades by Sebastian LaRocca. The family engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, and labor union corruption. It became defunct after the death of its last known made member in 2021.

descriptions: The Bufalino family is allied with the Pittsburgh crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Pittsburgh crime family

descriptions: Infobox lists Pittsburgh crime family as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Buffalo crime family, Cleveland crime family, Colombo crime family, Detroit Partnership, Genovese crime family, Los Angeles crime family, Patriarca crime family, Philadelphia crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Hells Angels MC

descriptions: Pittsburgh family fought a war with Cleveland family over Youngstown rackets after a period of cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Vincent 'Two-Gun Jimmy' Prato, the local caporegime from the Pittsburgh family, attempted to establish a monopoly over gambling and extortion rackets in Youngstown, a territory which had historically been shared between the two families. This resulted in a mob war, consisting of twelve murders, which lasted between 1978 and 1981.

descriptions: Cleveland family had a profit-sharing arrangement with Pittsburgh family over Youngstown rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: an arrangement with the Pittsburgh crime family which entitled the Cleveland family to twenty-five percent of the Pittsburgh family's profits from rackets in Youngstown.

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are mentioned alongside Pony Down as comparable Detroit gangs, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Like other Detroit street gangs, such as their Westside Detroit counterparts in the late 1970s; the Nasty Flynns (later the NF Bangers), and 7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs or the drug consortiums of the 1980s such as Young Boys Inc., Pony Down, Best Friends, Black Mafia Family and the Chambers Brothers, the Errol Flynns grew out of the racial and economic unrest that transformed Detroit in the late 1960s and 1970s.

descriptions: Pony Down rose to prominence after YBI's downfall, indicating a successor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Many gangs, such as "Best Friends", "Pony Down", " Black Mafia Family ", and " The Chambers Brothers ", rose to prominence in the wake of the YBI

Kings Crew Motorcycle Club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club based in Western Canada, founded in Calgary, Alberta in 1977. It became one of the dominant clubs in Alberta, participating in the Alberta Biker Conflict. The club engaged in drug trafficking, murder, assault, and extortion. It eventually joined the Hells Angels in the late 1990s.

descriptions: The Grim Reapers were one of four dominant clubs in Alberta, alongside Rebels, Warlords, and King's Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Along with the Rebels, the Warlords, and King's Crew, they were one of the four dominant outlaw motorcycle clubs operating in Alberta prior to 1997.

'Ndrina Sia.description: The 'Ndrina Sia is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Soverato, Calabria, Italy, with influence in neighboring towns. It is allied with other 'ndrine and was involved in a bloody feud from 2008 to 2010 that resulted in about twenty deaths. The clan has been targeted by multiple police operations, including Showdown, Showdown 2, and Showdown 3, leading to arrests and asset seizures. Its boss Vittorio Sia was killed in 2010.

descriptions: Lettsom G'z is a subset of Peckham Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: By the mid-2000s, the names of the various subsets had begun to change again and be replaced by SN1 (Spare No-1, formerly Peckham Boys, who were now the older generation), PYG (Pecknarm Young Gunners, formerly YYPB), SI (Shoot Instant, formerly YPB), OPB (Original Peckham Boys), DFA (Don't Fuck Around or Drugz Fundz Armz), CBM (Crane Block Massive), YBM (Yellow Brick Massive), PK (Pecknarm Killaz), and the Lettsom G'z (L.G).

descriptions: Outcast Motorcycle Club is listed as an enemy of Wheels of Soul.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemies of the Wheels of Soul include the Outcast Motorcycle Club, Sin City Titans, Hells Lovers Motorcycle Club and the Street Soldiers.

descriptions: Wheels of Soul plotted to kill members of Outcast Motorcycle Club in 2011.
edges.evidence_quote: At the direction of Colorado chapter president Jerry 'Shakka' Elkins, Fry and another Wheels of Soul member, Rasheed Jamal 'Diamond' Brandon, traveled to East St. Louis, Illinois to kill members of the Outkast motorcycle gang in January 2011.

descriptions: Outcast Motorcycle Club suspected of killing a Wheels of Soul member in 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: On August 30, 2014, Wheels of Soul member Steven Hicks was fatally gunned down while attending a party in Birmingham, Alabama... It is suspected that members of the rival Outcast Motorcycle Club had shown up to the event with firearms and opened fire.

Fullerton Boys.description: A gang primarily composed of Korean-American delinquents based in Fullerton, California, founded in 1996. Members originally belonged to other gangs such as LVM, SZA, and Wah Ching before adopting the Fullerton Boys name. The gang operates in the Orange Thorpe area and is known for claiming territory against rivals.

descriptions: Fullerton Boys members were originally from LVM.
edges.evidence_quote: Most of its members belonged to the LVM gang, SZA gang (Sarzana) gang and some from the Wah Ching gang.

descriptions: Fullerton Boys members were originally from SZA.
edges.evidence_quote: Most of its members belonged to the LVM gang, SZA gang (Sarzana) gang and some from the Wah Ching gang.

descriptions: Fullerton Boys members were originally from Wah Ching.
edges.evidence_quote: Most of its members belonged to the LVM gang, SZA gang (Sarzana) gang and some from the Wah Ching gang.

Fourth Reich (New Zealand gang).description: A racist skinhead prison gang formed in Paparua Prison, Christchurch, New Zealand in the early 1990s. It terrorized communities in Nelson and the West Coast of the South Island, and was perceived as a credible terrorist threat during the early 2000s. There is little evidence that the gang still exists.

descriptions: Fourth Reich is listed as a rival of Lost Breed.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Fourth Reich (New Zealand gang)
